thread.title: Former Ripon varsity boys basketball coach facing sex crime charges involving a minor bound for trial
thread.title_full: Former Ripon varsity boys basketball coach facing sex crime charges involving a minor bound for trial

author: @WBAY
title: Former Ripon varsity boys basketball coach facing sex crime charges involving a minor bound for trial
text:
Former Ripon varsity boys basketball coach facing sex crime charges involving a minor bound for trial
RIPON, Wis. (WBAY) - A former boys basketball coach at the Ripon Area School District who is charged with multiple sex crime charges involving sexual misconduct with a minor is now bound over for trial.
Online court records show Mihai Schnell-Harrison was ordered to stand trial during a court hearing Friday.
According to [an informational release](https://www.ripon.k12.wi.us/cms_files/resources/Press%20Release%2011-14-24%20Coach%20Arrested.pdf) from the Ripon Area School District sent to families on Thursday, Mihai Schnell-Harrison, who coached varsity boys basketball for one season at Ripon High School, was arrested by the Ripon Police Department on Tuesday.
On Friday, Mihai Schnell-Harrison was formally charged in Fond du Lac County with Child Enticement-Sexual Contact, Sex Assault of Student by School Staff, Exposing Genitals/Pubic Area/Intimate Parts to a Child, Possession of Child Pornography, and Expose Child to Harmful Material.
According to the criminal complaint filed Friday, Schnell-Harrison began communicating with a student at Ripon High School, first in a group chat and then later exclusively on Snapchat. The student is 16 years old. Police confirmed that Schnell-Harrison at least knew that the teen was a student at the high school where he worked.
When questioned by Ripon Police, the complaint said the student told them the conversations began to escalate into a sexual nature and they began sending nude pictures of themselves to each other. At one point, the student told police that he and Schnell-Harrison engaged in a sex act. The student later blocked Schnell-Harrison on Snapchat.
When Schnell-Harrison was questioned by police, Schnell-Harrison indicated he thought the student was 18. The criminal complaint points out that Schnell-Harrison signed a document when hired to Ripon Area School District, that reminds employees to have no sexual relations with students.
The criminal complaint said Schnell-Harrison told police he met the victim on Grindr and that the victim told him he was 18, but the complaint said that turned out to be a lie and he never met the victim on Grindr.
In Fond du Lac County court on Friday, Schnell-Harrison’s cash bond was set at $150,000. He was ordered to have no contact with anyone from the Ripon Area School District, no contact with the victim or their family, no contact with anyone under the age of 18, and no use of Snapchat.
His preliminary hearing is scheduled for November 22 at 2:00 p.m.
The Ripon Area School District included a statement in their informational release issued Thursday, saying:
“The District wants to thank the City of Ripon Police Department for their swift action over the past two days to ensure the safety and well-being of our students and staff. The safety of all students and staff is the District’s top priority. As such, please remind your child(ren) if they experience anything or hear anything that makes them feel uncomfortable, they should tell a parent or a trusted adult who can help report the information to the proper authorities. Parents too are reminded to report anything they see or hear that causes concern to the proper authorities. The District’s commitment to ensuring a safe, supportive, and respectful environment for all students and staff remains unwavering. We are committed to transparency and will continue to update the community within the bounds of privacy laws and the ongoing investigation. The City of Ripon Police Department reminded the District that due to the ongoing investigation, and the sensitive nature of the case involving a minor, information is limited at this time.”
According to Schnell-Harrison’s [bio on the Ripon Basketball website](https://www.riponbasketball.com/page/show/8229385-ripon-high-school-boys-basketball-coaching-staff), he started as an assistant coach at Ripon High School in 2016 before joining the Waupun boys basketball program from 2017 to 2019. He then joined Appleton East’s varsity boys basketball program as an assistant coach while also coaching the freshman team before returning to Ripon as the head boys basketball coach.
